title: Try installing and running Zed on your device using package managers.

items.correct: Checks for the presence of a binary in worktree and either returns its path or constructs an environment variable to run another script with language server ID. Then caches this new binary's path and creates command using it.
distractors: Generates a Git command to find the previous commit hash of a specified tag and outputs it for further processing.
Prepares and returns a session task or an error if loading sessions are unsupported.
Creates a JSON object from parsed keys and values along the specified path, handling nulls and nested structures.

items.correct: Converts multi-line string to a single line, removing certain characters and joining trimmed parts with commas.
distractors: Formats a string representing the range from `start` to `end`, adding one for Rust's zero-indexing.
Updates the function body to print "world" instead of "hello".
Extracts the outline structure of JavaScript functions in provided text.

items.correct: Generates a string representing the OS and architecture combination in release format.
distractors: Resets the title bar visibility of a window on macOS version 10.14 or later to be transparent by using an OS-level API call in Rust.

items.code:
let comments = self
    .github_client
    .get_pull_request_comments(&Repository::ZED, pull_request.number)
    .await?;
let qualifying_comments = comments
    .into_iter()
    .filter(|comment| Self::is_qualifying_approval(comment, pull_request))
    .collect_vec();
Ok(
    qualifying_comments
        .is_empty()
        .not()
        .then_some(ReviewSuccess::ApprovingComment(qualifying_comments)),
)
items.correct: Determines if a GitHub pull request contains any approving comments and returns success status based on that.
distractors: Prepares and returns a session task or an error if loading sessions are unsupported.
Converts a string to Windows PowerShell format with appropriate quoting and escaping.
Updates the current message phase, converts it to a string if not None and returns an empty vector when there are no reasons for reasoning.

items.correct: Resets navigation and UI components and updates user interface.
